channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Black Forest Labs FLUX 3 - Multimodal Flow Models that beat Seedance 2.0, Gemini Omni and Grok Imagine, and FLUX-mimic video-action robotics model
item.description: [AINews] Black Forest Labs FLUX 3 - Multimodal Flow Models that beat Seedance 2.0, Gemini Omni and Grok Imagine, and FLUX-mimic video-action robotics model

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What is Good? Extracting and Testing Implicit Theories of Literary Quality from LLM Reasoning Traces
item.description: LLMs evaluate literary quality by prioritizing voice and intentionality over correctness, based on analysis of reasoning traces and systematic text degradation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Knowledge Injection Exists in MoE? Exploring Expert-Aware Contrast Decoding in MoE for Mitigating LLMs'Hallucinations
item.description: Study proposes expert-aware contrastive decoding for mixture-of-experts models that uses expert activation pattern differences to mitigate language model hallucinations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Is MoE Routing a Huffman Code? Discovering the Frequency-Diversity Law in Chain-of-Thought
item.description: MoE routing follows Huffman Coding principles via the Frequency-Diversity Law, allocating sparse experts to frequent tokens and diverse experts to rare tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Human-in-the-Loop Large Language Model Framework for Identification of Cutaneous Immune-Related Adverse Events
item.description: LLM-driven framework with human oversight improved detection of cutaneous immune-related adverse events, achieving higher accuracy and inter-rater agreement than manual review while halving review time.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] More Is Not More: What Matters for Diversity in LLM Opinions?
item.description: Initial persona conditioning captures most LLM opinion diversity gains; additional details don't help monotonically, and different architectures explore non-overlapping opinion regions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LLM-INSTRUCT at UZH Shared Task 2026: Constraint-Aware Retrieval and Selective Debate for Paragraph-Level Argument Mining
item.description: LLM-INSTRUCT won the UZH Shared Task 2026 for argument mining using constraint-aware retrieval and selective debate with small language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Skill-Contracted Agents for Evidence-Aware Materials Literature Analysis
item.description: AlphaAgent framework uses explicit skill contracts to separate retrieval and report generation for materials science literature analysis, outperforming baseline systems in evaluation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Position: Natural Language Should Not Fully Replace Formal Languages
item.description: Research proves that beyond a specificity threshold, formal language specification is more efficient than expressing requirements in natural language.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Moir: Let the Model Direct Its Own Story for Robust Cross-Domain Knowledge Editing
item.description: Moir prevents reasoning degradation during knowledge editing by estimating covariance from the model's own decoding distribution.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Break Through the Compression Bottleneck: From Theory to Practice
item.description: Researchers demonstrate low-rank decomposition and quantization are non-orthogonal in LLM compression, propose Diagonal Adhesive Method to mitigate performance loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Making Open-Source Text LLM Watermarks Durable Against Merging
item.description: New adversarial training algorithm embeds durable watermarks in open-source LLMs that survive model merging operations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Routing Subspaces: Auditing Evaluation-to-Deployment Mismatch in Fine-Tuned Language Models
item.description: Study reveals fine-tuned models can pass safety evaluations while failing in deployment, then locates and corrects these mismatches via activation analysis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] TopoGuard: Graph Theory Based Defenses Against Split-Knowledge Attacks on RAG
item.description: TopoGuard defends RAG systems against split-knowledge attacks by using graph theory to detect malicious document combinations invisible to per-document filters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Preference Tuning as Spectral Update Reorganization
item.description: RLHF updates show spectral head-tail organization where compact heads carry endpoint shifts while heterogeneous tails are necessary for full learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AsymVerify at SemEval-2026 Task 6: Asymmetric Confidence-Gated Verification for Political Evasion Detection
item.description: Confidence-gated verification system AsymVerify achieves 0.85 Macro F1, placing 2nd in SemEval-2026 Task 6 for political evasion detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Answer-then-Edit: Reasoning Skeleton Editing for Anti-Distillation with Preserved Utility
item.description: New anti-distillation technique edits reasoning traces post-hoc to defend proprietary LLMs from distillation while maintaining answer accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Belief Propagation in LLM World Models: Measuring Strategic Information Bias with Prediction Markets
item.description: LLMs and prediction markets show English news biases territorial predictions about Ukraine by 64-72%, with the bias originating in source text, not the models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Naver-News-KO: A Korean News Summarization Dataset for Open-Source Fine-Tuning of Summarization Models
item.description: Naver-News-KO, a Korean news summarization dataset of 27,400 pairs, has been publicly available since January 2023 and receives approximately 33,000 monthly downloads.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GLAN-QnA-KR: A Seedless Taxonomy-Driven Korean Instruction Corpus
item.description: Researchers release GLAN-QnA-KR, a 303,581-row Korean instruction corpus with minimal exact and near-duplicate entries.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Confidently Deceptive: How Confidence Amplifies the Risk of LLM Deception
item.description: LLMs deliver high-confidence deceptive responses that humans prefer 78% of the time; misalignment fine-tuning amplifies this alignment risk.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SCoPE: Shift-Aware Speaker-Conditioned Priors for Emotion Recognition in Conversations
item.description: SCoPE improves emotion recognition in conversations by modeling speaker-specific emotional patterns and fusing multimodal signals with emotion shift prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Distinguishing Artificial from Authentic: Evaluating LLMs for Detecting LLM-Generated Content
item.description: LLMs show varying ability to detect their own generated content, performing well on programming tasks but poorly on short-answer questions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] thaulab@EEUCA 2026: Who Said What to Whom? A Targeting-Aware Neural-Symbolic Pipeline for Gaming Toxicity Detection
item.description: Ensemble transformer models with linguistic mediation achieved first-place accuracy in gaming chat toxicity detection by targeting underrepresented harmful classes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Domyn-Small: A European 10B Reasoning Language Model
item.description: Domyn-Small, a 10B open-weight reasoning model, achieves strong accuracy on math and reasoning benchmarks while using substantially fewer tokens than comparable models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Storyteller in the Model: Narrative Pattern Inheritance, Escalation Dynamics, and Alignment Governance in LLMs
item.description: LLMs absorb narrative storytelling patterns from training data, leading to behavioral drift including sycophancy and deceptiveness in extended interactions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ShriNep@EEUCA 2026: RAKSHAK - Multi-Task DeBERTa with Rationale Distillation and Jigsaw-Augmented Training for Toxic Intent Classification
item.description: Multi-task DeBERTa combining rationale distillation and Jigsaw data augmentation classifies toxic gaming chat messages into six fine-grained intent categories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Semantic Field Theory: Historical Origin, Higher-Order Interaction, and Stabilized Semantic Inference
item.description: Paper formalizes Semantic Field Theory into mathematical framework for lexical semantics using semantic fields, contextual deformation, and higher-order interactions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Knowledge-Injection Framework for Zero-Shot Adaptation of LLMs to Delirium Prediction
item.description: Inference-time knowledge injection significantly improves LLaMA models' performance on ICU delirium prediction without fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Response drift across frontier large language models
item.description: All frontier LLMs show response drift from expert references, with magnitude varying by model and domain, requiring human-centered evaluation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RE-AD: Real-Time Requirement Adherence for Data Labeling
item.description: LLM-based framework validates crowdsourced annotations in real-time via SOP decomposition, achieving 0.749 F1 score with 82% production error acceptance rate.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] DataPrep-Bench: Benchmarking LLMs as Training Data Preparators
item.description: DataPrep-Bench introduces the first unified benchmark for evaluating LLM-driven data preparation capabilities including data construction and quality evaluation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PhantomFill: When the Form Demands an Answer, Language Models Invent One
item.description: Required form fields cause language models to hallucinate answers instead of admitting insufficient information.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Active Ingredient in Muon's Grokking
item.description: Muon optimizer reaches grokking threshold faster than AdamW due to orthogonalization via Newton-Schulz iteration rather than spectral-norm constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Scaling Closed-Loop Feature Channel Configuration with LLMs
item.description: LLMs optimize neural network channel widths at scale, achieving better accuracy and parameter efficiency on CIFAR-100.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Multimodal CoLRAG-TF: Triple-Filtered Retrieval for Complex PDFs
item.description: Multimodal CoLRAG-TF fuses text embeddings, keyword matching, knowledge-graph triples, and image similarity to improve retrieval-augmented generation for complex PDFs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Adaptive Depth in Looped Transformers: Diagnosing Learned Halting Gates and Trajectory Readouts
item.description: Study of looped Transformers shows adaptive depth performance limited by trajectory formation during training rather than halting gate expressivity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Generative Bayesian Filtering for State Estimation
item.description: Proposed Generative Bayesian Filtering uses conditional VAEs to improve state estimation for nonlinear dynamic systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Do Active SAE Feature Planes Carry More Holonomy? A Preregistered Reversal in Gemma
item.description: Study finds active SAE feature planes in Gemma 2 carry less holonomy than mixed-feature controls, falsifying the prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Uncertainty-Aware Trust Estimation for Multi-LLM Systems via Structured Expert Judgement
item.description: Research uses uncertainty-aware trust estimation via Cooke weighting to improve LLM ensemble aggregation with heterogeneous models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] CLOE: Christoffel Loss Autoencoder for Anomaly Detection
item.description: CLOE combines autoencoders with Christoffel Function-based detection for improved semi-supervised anomaly detection in high-dimensional data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Position: Stop Reactively Patching Your Model Every Time and Start Proactive Test-Driven AI Development
item.description: Proactive test-driven AI development requires fewer iterations than reactive model patching for better system generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Grounding Investor Views: Neural Predicates in the Black-Litterman Model
item.description: Neural predicates provide a structured, data-driven method for generating investor views in the Black-Litterman portfolio construction model.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Graph Neural Network approach to zero-shot Digital Twins
item.description: A physics-informed Graph Neural Network framework enables zero-shot digital twins that adapt to different geometries and boundary conditions without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ReliableTableQA:How Much Supervision Does Reliability Annotation Need?
item.description: ReliableTableQA trains LLMs to flag statistically unreliable SQL query results in tabular QA, achieving high reliability detection with only 200 supervised examples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Codec-Gauge: Learning Compression-Friendly Gauges for Transformer KV Caches
item.description: Codec-Gauge learns orthogonal channel transforms for Transformer KV-cache compression, reducing KL divergence 44% average versus raw coordinates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Leveraging Biokinetic Knowledge Priors for Data-Scarce Bioprocess Modeling
item.description: Neural networks pre-trained on simulated biokinetic models match domain-structured approaches for bioprocess prediction with minimal experimental data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Atoms to Entropy: Optimal Noise Allocation for Diffusion Training in the Convex Regime
item.description: Diffusion model training achieves optimality using finitely many noise levels, with allocation proportional to generative entropy rate.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] HypNO: A Graph-Based Neural Operator with Physics-Informed Message Passing for Hyperbolic Conservation Laws
item.description: HypNO, a graph-based neural operator with physics-informed message passing, accurately predicts solutions to hyperbolic conservation laws including shocks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Improving Access to Essential Medicines via Decision-Aware Machine Learning
item.description: Decision-aware machine learning framework improved essential medicines allocation in Sierra Leone, increasing product consumption by 19% in treatment districts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When RLVR Shrinks the Reasoning Boundary: Diagnosing Pass@k Inversion
item.description: RLVR training improves pass@1 but worsens pass@k on boundary prompts; Per-Problem Base Anchoring preserves rare correct trajectories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Conflict Resolution under Degraded Surveillance in Air Corridors Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Multi-agent deep Q-learning resolves conflicts between autonomous aircraft operating under degraded surveillance in structured air corridors.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SOAP, Muon, and Beyond: Pushing LLM Pretraining Scales
item.description: Researchers enhance SOAP and Muon optimizers for stable large-scale LLM pretraining, outperforming AdamW on trillion-token models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SevDiff: Severity-Conditioned Diffusion for Long-Tail Conflict Trajectory Generation
item.description: SevDiff, a diffusion model, generates vehicle collision trajectories with specified Time-to-Collision values for ADAS evaluation, addressing rare conflict data scarcity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Beyond SBDD: Geometric Deep Learning in Polypharmacology and Multi-target Drug Design
item.description: Geometric deep learning enables computational design of drugs targeting multiple biological targets simultaneously for complex diseases.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SenCos-GEM: SENet-Calibrated and Law-of-Cosines-Constrained Geometry-Enhanced Molecular Representation for Property Prediction
item.description: Molecular representation learning framework combining physics-based geometric constraints with dynamic feature modulation for improved property prediction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Thermodynamic Weight Decay: Exploring Grokking Acceleration via Attention Specific Heat
item.description: CvAdamW optimizer uses attention-based thermodynamics to detect phase transitions and accelerate delayed neural network generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Double-Scoring: Reliable Extraction of Strong Lottery Tickets
item.description: Double-scoring improves extraction of strong lottery tickets by optimizing augmented score tensors, reducing sensitivity to sparsity hyperparameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Monkey King Bang: A Unified Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model
item.description: MKB is a unified scientific multimodal foundation model supporting understanding and generation across six domains including biology, chemistry, earth science, and medical imaging.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] StabilityBench: Benchmarking Instability in LLMs
item.description: StabilityBench converts static LLM evaluations to multi-turn conversations, revealing significant performance degradation with demographic proxies and sycophantic variations.
